what_it_does: A posted note by Tim that comments on another board item, endorsing a proposed mechanism for deterministic code annotation anchoring using text_quote, prefix/suffix, code:// URI, and line ranges, with a loud failure notice when the annotation no longer matches its target.
observations: Note type 'note' in state 'posted' authored by tim in channel dragnet-development
Links to item-79c533e9 via a 'commented_on' relation
Body quotes a prior statement about converting a goal into a mechanism
Mentions deterministic re-anchoring and a standing no-silent-failures rule
Tim explicitly rejects 'frozen pointers' as not good
standouts: Strong endorsement of deterministic, context-rich code annotation anchoring over frozen pointers
Explicit alignment with the no-silent-failures rule
conventions: Uses standard noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Markdown body uses a [point] tag and inline quotation
References another item through a structured link
notes: Tim's note agreeing that deterministic, context-aware code annotations with loud mismatch failures are preferable to frozen pointers.
questions: What exact shape does the proposed anchoring mechanism take?
What does 'frozen pointers' specifically refer to in this codebase?
How and where is the mismatch notice surfaced to the agent?
What is the content of the referenced item-79c533e9?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a commentary note supporting deterministic annotation anchoring, which is exactly what it does.
apparent_themes: Reducing agent effort to locate and update code
intention_signals: To validate and reinforce a design direction where code annotations are robustly re-anchored to their targets and explicitly notify users when they drift, rather than silently editing the wrong location.
novelty: Frames the shift from a stated goal to a concrete mechanism, combining text_quote + prefix/suffix + code:// + line range with a loud mismatch notice, and explicitly rejects 'frozen pointers'.
contribution: Preserves a stakeholder endorsement and concise rationale for context-rich, fail-loud code annotation anchoring as a design signal.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim endorsing deterministic code annotation anchoring via text_quote, prefix/suffix, code:// URI, and line ranges with loud failure on mismatch, rejecting frozen pointers.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's agreement with and reinforcement of a no-silent-failure, deterministic re-anchoring approach for code annotations.
